High School
Undergraduate

The Molecular Sciences Institute is committed to training the next generation of scientists. Use the link below to see a list of resources for high school students or continue on this page to see the two opportunities we are currently offering.


Links to webpages and organizations offering education as well as internship opportunities to high school students and undergrads:

Genomics Resources for High School Students and Undergrads.

Molecular Sciences Institute
Center for Genomic Experimentation and Computation
Genomics Research Summer Internships 2004
for High School Juniors and Seniors
The Molecular Sciences Institute in Berkeley, CA is again offering talented high school juniors and seniors a summer work experience in genomics.

MSI is an independent, nonprofit research laboratory located on Shattuck Avenue above the Berkeley BART station. MSI is committed to training, motivating and mentoring future genomic scientists. Our Genomic Sciences Internship Program provides Bay Area high school juniors and seniors an opportunity to gain hands-on experience in a genomics laboratory for eight weeks each summer. These competitive internships are sponsored by the National Human Genome Research Institute.

MSI combines genomic experimentation with computer modeling. The mission of MSI is to predict the behavior of cells and organisms in response to defined genetic and environmental changes. Progress toward this goal will significantly increase our understanding of biological systems and help catalyze radical changes in how diseases are understood and treated.

These 8-week internships are open to students who are high school juniors or seniors during the 2003-2004 academic year. After receiving a comprehensive introduction to genomics at MSI, students will select a research area and partner with an MSI scientist to complete a research project, written report, and oral presentation. Eighty percent of a student's time will be spent at MSI doing research, learning about the backgrounds of our collaborators during "Meet the Scientists" presentations, participating in genomics workshops, and attending MSI team meetings. Twenty percent of a student's time will be spent on MSI-sponsored scientific enrichment activities and fieldtrips throughout the Bay Area to supplement the student's learning.

Interns will be paid $15 an hour. After completing the summer internship, students may also take advantage of ongoing individualized tutoring in science and mathematics provided by MSI throughout the following school year. In addition, MSI will fund SAT preparation courses for the students, if applicable. Interns must be U.S. citizens or permanent residents.

Applications are due by April 1, 2004. Internships will begin June 14th and end on August 6th. Interested students should send completed applications via postal mail or email it to the address below. Please call 510/981-8738 or email internships@molsci.org with questions.

Molecular Sciences Institute
High School Student Internship
2168 Shattuck Avenue, 2nd Floor
Berkeley, CA 94704
internships@molsci.org
Phone 510/981-8738"

The Summer 2003 internship is completed. To learn about the talented students that were last summer's interns, please follow the link below.

MSI Summer Internship 2003


High School Science Fairs

Students, have you ever participated in a science fair? If not, you are missing out on a wonderfully rewarding experience. Let us help you get started!!! Please visit our partner at Science Buddies for all the details. We encourage you to try the genomics experiments listed in the "Cutting Edge" section. We are here to help you do this -- so please take a look!
